lede: The 20-week roadmap, distilled to the 8 highest-leverage weeks for shipping production AI applications. ~10–12 hrs/week, build-first. Skips the deep theory and JVM-native detours — pure path to a deployed, agentic RAG capstone you can demo. Do the full roadmap if you want senior depth; do this if you need to be dangerous fast.

weeks.title: LLM Foundations & APIs
weeks.goal: Goal: get fluent with the raw building blocks — tokens, prompting, structured output, and tool use — calling a frontier API directly, no framework.

days.hands: Set up ai-fasttrack repo with uv; model a Pydantic request/response; one typed function with a failing-validation test.

exercise.goal: Set up a typed Python AI project.
steps: Create ai-fasttrack with uv; add a Pydantic request/response model.
Write one typed function with a failing-validation test.
acceptance: The validation test proves the model rejects bad input.
exercise.stretch: Add a FastAPI stub exposing the function.

days.hands: Tabulate $/M-token for 3 models you'll use; predict then verify token counts on 3 sample prompts.

steps: Tabulate $/M tokens for 3 models you'll use.
Predict then verify token counts on 3 prompts.
Note which model fits which job.
acceptance: A cost table for 3 models exists.
Predicted vs actual token counts are compared.
exercise.stretch: Add TTFT from a quick probe.

days.hands: Build an extractor: job description → {role, seniority, skills[], remote} validated as a Pydantic model.

steps: Build an extractor: job description → {role, seniority, skills[], remote} Pydantic model.
exercise.stretch: Add an abstain path when confidence is low.

days.hands: LLM that can call 3 tools — get_weather, search_files, run_sql — with a clean dispatch loop.

acceptance: The model selects the right tool per query.
Errors return to the model instead of crashing.

weeks.deliverable: Repo ft-week-01 — 3 raw-API scripts (summarize, extract, tool-use) + README on tokens & cost.

weeks.title: RAG Essentials
weeks.goal: Goal: build retrieval-augmented generation from scratch, then learn the vector-DB landscape and add reranking — the backbone of most production AI apps.

days.hands: Chunk a docs corpus 3 ways; embed with all-MiniLM-L6-v2; eyeball the tradeoffs.

exercise.goal: Pick a chunking strategy with evidence.
steps: Chunk a corpus 3 ways and embed with all-MiniLM-L6-v2.
acceptance: A 1-pager states which chunking wins and when.
A default is chosen with a reason.

days.hands: Ingest docs into Chroma; answer questions with citations; no framework yet.

exercise.goal: Build RAG from scratch with citations.
steps: Ingest docs into Chroma (raw SDK + chromadb).
Retrieve top-k and answer with sources.
acceptance: Questions are answered with cited sources.

days.hands: Swap Chroma → pgvector; record recall@5 / MRR; write a 1-pager picking a vector DB for millions of vectors + metadata filtering.

steps: Swap Chroma → pgvector; record recall@5 / MRR.
Score Chroma/pgvector/Qdrant/Weaviate/Pinecone for millions of vectors + metadata filtering.
acceptance: A decision matrix justifies a pick.

days.hands: Add a cross-encoder reranker; rebuild the pipeline in LlamaIndex; compare answer quality with/without rerank.

exercise.goal: Rerank and rebuild in a framework.
steps: Add a cross-encoder reranker; compare with/without.
acceptance: A measurable rerank improvement is recorded.
The framework version matches scratch behaviour.

weeks.deliverable: Repo ft-week-02 — RAG that answers with citations + a vector-DB decision matrix + recall numbers.

weeks.title: Agents & Tool Orchestration
weeks.goal: Goal: write a ReAct loop by hand, then rebuild it in the framework interviewers ask about (LangGraph) and expose tools via MCP.

days.hands: Write a ReAct agent (no framework) that uses your Week-2 RAG + a calculator tool; log every step.

steps: Implement reason→act→observe using your RAG + a calculator tool.
acceptance: The agent solves a multi-step task with tools.
Steps are logged; the loop terminates.
exercise.stretch: Add a retry on malformed tool calls.

days.hands: Rebuild the same agent in LangGraph with a conditional edge + memory; note what the framework hides.

steps: Model state, tools, and a conditional edge.
acceptance: The agent runs as a graph with memory.
A conditional edge routes on tool output.
exercise.stretch: Add a checkpointer and resume mid-run.

days.hands: Wrap one of your tools as an MCP server; call it from the agent through an MCP client.

steps: Wrap one tool as an MCP server.
Call it from the agent via an MCP client.
acceptance: The agent uses the tool through MCP.
exercise.stretch: Consume the server from a second client.

days.hands: Add a reflection step + a loop/cost circuit-breaker; compare quality vs the plain ReAct baseline.

steps: Add a reflection step + a loop/cost circuit-breaker.
acceptance: A comparison vs ReAct is recorded.

weeks.udemy: Resources: LangGraph docs + Anthropic agent guide (free).
weeks.deliverable: Repo ft-week-03 — RAG agent (scratch + LangGraph) + one MCP tool + reflection.

weeks.title: Evaluation & Observability
weeks.goal: Goal: the most undervalued skill — prove your system works. Build evals, an LLM-as-judge, tracing, and basic guardrails.

days.hands: Enumerate the failure modes you expect from your Week-3 agent; group them.

exercise.goal: List the failure modes you must catch.
steps: Enumerate expected failures of your agent.
exercise.stretch: Map each to a concrete test.

days.hands: Label ~50 traces → cluster failures → write an LLM-judge per mode → validate TPR/TNR → keep a golden set of 20 that gates CI.

steps: Write an LLM judge per mode and validate (TPR/TNR).
Keep a 20-item golden set that gates CI.

days.hands: Instrument every LLM/tool call with Langfuse; build a dashboard answering "which tool calls fail most?"

steps: Wire Langfuse into every call (cost, latency, tools).
Build a 'which tool fails most' dashboard.
acceptance: The dashboard answers the tool-failure question.

days.hands: Add input + output guardrails; run 10 injection attacks; document each defense.

acceptance: A red-team doc lists attacks + fixes.

weeks.udemy: Resources: Hamel + Eugene Yan + DLAI (audit free).
weeks.deliverable: Repo ft-week-04 — eval suite + validated judge + Langfuse dashboard + guardrails.

weeks.title: Fine-Tuning & Local Models
weeks.goal: Goal: know when fine-tuning helps (rarely) and how to do it — LoRA/QLoRA — plus running quantized models locally. Decision tree: prompt → few-shot → RAG → fine-tune.

days.hands: Write a 1-pager: for your capstone task, prove (or disprove) that prompting/RAG is enough before touching fine-tuning.

exercise.goal: Decide whether to fine-tune at all.
steps: Prove prompting/RAG is or isn't enough for your task.
Write the decision + the metric that would justify FT.
acceptance: A written decision with a justifying metric.
exercise.stretch: Sketch the labeling plan if you proceeded.

days.hands: LoRA-tune a small model (e.g. Llama-3.2-3B) on a focused dataset; beat the base model on a held-out test set.

exercise.goal: Run a LoRA fine-tune that beats base.
steps: LoRA-tune a small model (e.g. Llama-3.2-3B) on a focused set.
acceptance: Tuned beats base on the held-out metric.

days.hands: Run a quantized model locally with Ollama; compare quality + latency vs the frontier API on 10 prompts.

steps: Run a quantized model via Ollama.
Compare quality + latency vs the frontier API on 10 prompts.
exercise.stretch: Expose an OpenAI-compatible endpoint and reuse your ft0 script.

weeks.udemy: Resources: Hugging Face course (free) — pairs with the HF cert.
weeks.deliverable: Repo ft-week-05 — LoRA model beating base + "when would I fine-tune?" writeup.

weeks.title: Inference, Serving & Cost
weeks.goal: Goal: make it fast and cheap. Inference metrics, batching and caching, and a model gateway/router — the difference between a demo and something you can afford to run.

days.hands: Serve your Week-5 model with vLLM; benchmark TTFT / TPOT / throughput under load; build a latency-vs-cost table.

acceptance: Inference metrics are measured under load.

days.hands: Load-test continuous batching; chart throughput vs concurrency; enable speculative decoding and measure the speedup.

steps: Load-test continuous batching; chart throughput vs concurrency.
exercise.stretch: Find the concurrency that maximises goodput.

days.hands: Add prompt + semantic caching; route easy queries to a small model via a gateway; measure the $ saved.

steps: Route easy queries to a small model via a gateway.
acceptance: Routing works on a realistic mix.
exercise.stretch: Add cache invalidation and test it.

weeks.deliverable: Repo ft-week-06 — vLLM load-test + batching chart + cache/gateway + cost table.

weeks.title: Production Deployment & System Design
weeks.goal: Goal: ship it to the cloud and be able to whiteboard it. Reference architecture, AWS deploy, eval-gated CI/CD with IaC, and one AI system-design drill.

days.hands: Diagram your app as a layered architecture; label each layer in your actual code.

steps: Diagram your app's layers and label them in code.
acceptance: A layered diagram maps to your code.

days.hands: Containerize the app; deploy behind API Gateway + Lambda (or App Runner); call Bedrock as a model provider.

steps: Deploy behind API Gateway + Lambda (or App Runner).

days.hands: Terraform your infra; GitHub Actions pipeline that runs the Week-4 eval gate before any deploy.

steps: GitHub Actions runs the ft3 eval gate before deploy.
Confirm a regressing change is blocked.

resources.label: Eugene Yan — System Design for ML

days.hands: Write a 2-page design doc with real numbers: index size, latency budget, hybrid retrieval, caching, multi-tenant isolation, eval pipeline.

steps: Write a 2-page 'RAG search at scale' design with real numbers.
Cover index size, latency budget, hybrid retrieval, caching, multi-tenant isolation, eval.
exercise.stretch: Do a 30-min spoken mock of it.

weeks.udemy: Resources: AWS + Terraform docs (free) — pairs with the AWS AI Practitioner + Terraform certs.
weeks.deliverable: Repo ft-week-07 — deployed app on AWS + Terraform + eval-gated CI + 1 system-design doc.

weeks.title: Capstone & Portfolio
weeks.goal: Goal: assemble everything into one demo-able agentic RAG app, then make it legible to a hiring loop — README, blog post, and STAR stories.

resources.label: Ship an end-to-end agentic RAG app (your domain)

days.hands: Combine RAG (W2) + agent/MCP (W3) + evals (W4) + deploy (W7) into one deployed app you'd demo to a hiring manager. No tutorials — just build.

steps: Combine RAG (ft1) + agent/MCP (ft2) + evals (ft3) + deploy (ft6) into one deployed app.
acceptance: A deployed, demo-able agentic RAG app exists.

days.hands: Wire the eval gate into CI; upgrade the README with an architecture diagram, a 60-sec demo gif, and an eval scoreboard.

steps: Wire the eval gate into CI.
Upgrade the README: architecture diagram, 60-sec demo gif, eval scoreboard.

resources.label: Eugene Yan — Why you should write

days.hands: Write 3 STAR stories (each ends with a metric) + 1 blog post on the capstone; pin the repo and publish.

steps: Write 3 STAR stories (each ends in a metric).
Write 1 blog post on the capstone.
acceptance: The repo is pinned and presentable.

weeks.udemy: Resources: your own artifacts — no course this week.
weeks.deliverable: Deployed agentic RAG capstone + polished README + 1 blog post + 3 STAR stories.
